title: autoupdate annotation ignored in kube deployment yaml
body:
### Issue Description

Setting the `io.containers.autoupdate` annotation described in https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-auto-update.1.html#auto-updates-and-kubernetes-yaml has no effect for k8s deployments

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. create container with autoupdate label and check the container for the label
```
+ podman container create --name autoupdate-test --replace --label io.containers.autoupdate=registry quay.io/quay/busybox echo foobar
b60685c5d4940f50c21df16bdae6b22e54d99380a86ddcf85eb4601881d37d05
+ podman inspect --format '{{index .Config.Labels "io.containers.autoupdate"}}' autoupdate-test
registry
```
Label `io.containers.autoupdate` is set to `registry` as expected.
2. generate pod from the container and check the container for the label
```
+ podman kube generate -t pod autoupdate-test
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-5.0.2
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    io.containers.autoupdate/autoupdate-test: registry
  creationTimestamp: "2024-05-24T10:01:07Z"
  labels:
    app: autoupdate-test-pod
  name: autoupdate-test-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - command:
    - echo
    - foobar
    image: quay.io/quay/busybox:latest
    name: autoupdate-test

+ podman kube generate -t pod autoupdate-test | podman kube play --replace -
Pods stopped:
565caf998e763b8d6c519ffd328e66122ce0d2e2def9a5e1326e4068ded49427
Pods removed:
565caf998e763b8d6c519ffd328e66122ce0d2e2def9a5e1326e4068ded49427
Secrets removed:
Volumes removed:
Pod:
3bef015c0b43d19370eb52970c4e31231410f974445f51653d76535fd39be7a1
Container:
ca5311061251be18cc26487d7109af61b28c32b13d7f7daec7a6f928438e2bed

+ podman inspect --format '{{index .Config.Labels "io.containers.autoupdate"}}' autoupdate-test-pod-autoupdate-test
registry
```
Label `io.containers.autoupdate` is set to `registry` as expected.
3. generate deployment from the container and check the container for the label
```
+ podman kube generate -t deployment autoupdate-test
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-5.0.2
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2024-05-24T10:01:11Z"
  labels:
    app: autoupdate-test-pod
  name: autoupdate-test-pod-deployment
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: autoupdate-test-pod
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        io.containers.autoupdate/autoupdate-test: registry
      creationTimestamp: "2024-05-24T10:01:11Z"
      labels:
        app: autoupdate-test-pod
      name: autoupdate-test-pod
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - echo
        - foobar
        image: quay.io/quay/busybox:latest
        name: autoupdate-test

+ podman kube generate -t deployment autoupdate-test | podman kube play --replace -
Pods stopped:
f81601b9f49148c56e8cb70a1f99b9cd7314582eb5968dbc728a3b1e857fe048
Pods removed:
f81601b9f49148c56e8cb70a1f99b9cd7314582eb5968dbc728a3b1e857fe048
Secrets removed:
Volumes removed:
Pod:
7145dc532b82d504d653266dc3570c37abed42c9839bbe701bdabdcefa054bc1
Container:
bbe5afb016c8518bc83180573e62fa3ba087c5ce50bf1831970dd608eda42a1a

+ podman inspect --format '{{index .Config.Labels "io.containers.autoupdate"}}' autoupdate-test-pod-deployment-pod-autoupdate-test
```

Label `io.containers.autoupdate` is missing!

### Describe the results you received

The autoupdate label is not set despite the annotation in the kube yaml.
According to https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/20675 the label should be set at the pod level, but that does not seem to be what `podman kube play` is doing:
```
+ podman inspect autoupdate-test-pod | grep -F 'io.containers.autoupdate'
+ podman inspect autoupdate-test-pod-autoupdate-test | grep -F 'io.containers.autoupdate'
    "io.containers.autoupdate": "registry"
    "io.containers.autoupdate/autoupdate-test": "registry",
+ podman inspect autoupdate-test-pod-deployment-pod | grep -F 'io.containers.autoupdate'
+ podman inspect autoupdate-test-pod-deployment-pod-autoupdate-test | grep -F 'io.containers.autoupdate'
```

### Describe the results you expected

`io.containers.autoupdate` label should be set correctly

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
